title: Food & Restaurants
shortSummary: Order food and drinks, talk about taste, and enjoy a meal out in Mandarin.
culturalNote: Eating is central to Chinese social life, and dishes are usually shared in the middle of the table rather than ordered individually. It is polite to invite others to eat first and to keep refilling each other's tea. Tipping is not expected in mainland China, and paying happens by saying 买单 (mǎidān). Mobile payment with WeChat or Alipay is the norm almost everywhere.

grammar.explanation: Attach 好 (hǎo) to a verb to mean 'good to do': 好吃 (good to eat, delicious), 好喝 (good to drink), 好看 (good to look at, nice).
grammar.commonMistake: Using 好吃 for drinks. Food is 好吃; drinks are 好喝.

grammar.explanation: 要 (yào) means 'want' and is the natural verb for ordering: 我要一碗面 (I want a bowl of noodles). Remember the measure word between the number and the noun.

examples.en: I want a cup of tea.
We want two bowls of rice.

grammar.commonMistake: Dropping the measure word: it is 一碗面, not 一面.

dialogue.setup: Da Wei and a waiter at a busy noodle restaurant.

lines.speaker: 大伟
lines.pinyin: Hǎo, wǒ yào yì wǎn, bú yào tài là.
lines.english: Good, I'll have a bowl, not too spicy.

lines.speaker: 大伟
lines.english: A cup of tea, thank you.

quiz.question: How do you say 'The bill, please'?
quiz.explanation: 买单 (mǎidān) asks for the bill.

quiz.question: 这个茶很好_____。 (delicious to drink)
quiz.explanation: Drinks use 好喝; food uses 好吃.

quiz.question: Arrange: 茶 / 要 / 我 / 一杯
quiz.explanation: Subject 我, verb 要, then 一杯茶 (a cup of tea).
